creator: draganj84@gmail.com
text:
Gigabit Ethernet quad-port NIC card model "Mikrotik RouterBoard44 GV" crashes/freezes kernel every time either immediately after first packets flow trough it or after some time (5-10 minuts max.).

Tested on FreeBSD 7.4-RELEASE amd64, FreeBSD 8.1-RELEASE amd64, FreeBSD 8.2-RELEASE amd64.
Tested on three different computers with very different specs (AMD and Intel, 4GB RAM to 12 GB RAM), and configurations. 
Tested with 3 different cards to make sure the card is not faulty.
Tested with 2 different routers to make sure they are not faulty in some way.
As a note, it works fine on linux on Windows machines. This rules out possibility that model is fault in some way.

Card is using default vge driver.

On 7.4-RELEASE the kernel crash shows that it crashed (trap 14 or trap 28). On 8.x-RELEASE computer freezes and the only way is to restart it.

Output of pciconf -lbcv and dmesg from one of machines is attached in the same file output.txt.

Fix: No known way to fix issue.

Patch attached with submission follows:
How-To-Repeat: Crash is reproducible 100% of time.
Seems that kernel crashes immediately after running dhcp on one of ports to acquire IPv4 address.

#ifconfig vge0 up
#dhclient vge0

And machine freezes almost immediately.

If address is assigned statically, there is no immediate crash but it will follow as soon as some traffic flows trough (surfing the web).
